name: Vijay Iyer
description: American composer, pianist, bandleader, producer, and writer
abstract: Vijay Iyer ([ˌvɪdʒeɪ ˈaɪjər]; born Vijay Raghunathan, October 26, 1971) is a composer, pianist, bandleader, producer, writer, and professor based in New York City. The New York Times has called him a "social conscience, multimedia collaborator, system builder, rhapsodist, historical thinker and multicultural gateway".

concerts.label: Zakir Hussain Eternal: Celebrating 75 Years of Genius
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: We were talking about Jason as the next – the younger generation. Then Vijay Iyer now . . . That’s what I mean. When these younger ones get as old as Danilo and Rubalcaba, they’re going to be even better.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Oh. Vijay Iyer. He’s a piano player. And then Rudresh Mahanthappa. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: David Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: And 25 years later a friend of mine went and Vijay Iyer was the director of the thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: A.B. Spellman
about_quotes.evidence: People like Vijay Iyer I think brought some newer stuff to it.
about_quotes.doc_title: A.B. Spellman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 19, 2022
